author: codex-root

patch.value: Research against Monica 4.x commit 32028ce and the actual CLI catalog found that OpenAI and Anthropic exports omit argument-only leaf commands and required positional arguments, while the safety heuristic advertises contacts introduction, contacts avatar, and attachment uploads as read-only. Harden the shared command contract so generated tools are lossless, leaf-only, schema-valid, and mutation-safe across OpenAI, Anthropic, MCP, command-catalog, and safe-commands outputs.
Make generated agent tool contracts executable and mutation-safe
Make every generated agent tool correspond to an executable Monica CLI leaf, preserve positional and inherited inputs, and classify every Monica data mutation as unsafe for read-only planners.

OpenAI and Anthropic exports include every executable leaf command exactly once; Required and optional positional arguments appear in tool input schemas with stable names and correct requiredness; Inherited global and parent options remain available without duplicate parameters; Every remote write command is excluded from agent-tools safe-commands, including introduction, avatar, document upload, and photo upload; All exported contracts validate against documented schemas in JSON and default TOON output; Repo-wide release gates retain exact 100/100/100/100 coverage, 100 percent docstrings, <=300 code lines, and zero dependency vulnerabilities

Authoritative Monica 4.x route source and current generated command tree inspected; duplicate-safe all-status pm search and fuzzy dedupe audit completed; private settings remain outside the repository in read-only mode.
Agents can invoke Monica deterministically without missing required IDs or being told that write operations are safe.
Prevents unsafe mutation planning and unlocks complete OpenAI, Anthropic, and MCP tool execution for all Monica resources.
Every generated tool is executable from its schema and every safe-command entry is guaranteed not to mutate Monica data.
Live API research exposed contract errors in the agent interface even though endpoint parity is complete.

Run monica --json info command-catalog and observe contacts introduction/avatar plus documents/photos upload marked read; run monica --json agent-tools openai and observe argument-only leaf tools/required positional IDs are absent.
Agent exports contain every executable leaf with complete arguments/options and classify all Monica mutations as writes.
Exports omit positional arguments and some leaves, and safety metadata advertises several writes as read-only.
Agent planners can generate unexecutable commands or select mutating commands from the purported read-only allow-list.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Authoritative Monica 4.x branch remains pinned at 32028ce with 35 mapped resources and 172 endpoints. The local instance is unavailable at bootstrap due to Cloudflare trust-proxy loading, so research used official source plus local deterministic catalogs and only GET probes.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Do not store credentials or live response bodies in pm. ~/.monica-cli/settings.json is mode 0600, uses default TOON, and has readOnlyMode=true. Preserve it byte-for-byte except CLI-owned cache refreshes.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Endpoint parity is not agent-contract parity: a CLI can map every route yet still produce incomplete tool schemas or unsafe planning metadata.

patch.value: OpenAI Anthropic and MCP command behavior

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Implementation milestone: all 233 executable leaves are exported exactly once across OpenAI, Anthropic, and MCP. Positional arguments, inherited options, Commander mandatory inputs, boolean flags, and drain-aware large output are verified. The safe allow-list contains 124 commands and excludes all identified remote/local mutations. Vitest is exact 100/100/100/100 across 845 tests.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Design decision: tool schemas inherit global and parent options with leaf-long-flag override semantics, omit root --version because it exits instead of executing the leaf, and use Commander mandatory metadata rather than help-description heuristics.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Real CLI pipes are required acceptance evidence for large agent outputs: mocked console logging concealed 64 KiB truncation even after structural unit tests passed.

Latest @unbrained/pm-cli and pm-changelog are both 2026.7.22, including the managed project extension.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: The live Monica instance remains unavailable because its Cloudflare trusted-proxy bootstrap fails before authentication. This is an operator-side server condition already covered by monica-server-diagnostics, not a CLI parity failure; no new duplicate issue was created.

patch.value: All 233 tools are unique and executable across three exporters, all 124 safe commands are non-mutating, large JSON parses successfully, and exact 100/100/100/100 plus live read-only acceptance passes.

OpenAI, Anthropic, and MCP exporters now emit all 233 unique executable leaves with positional, inherited, typed, and authoritative mandatory inputs; drain-aware output prevents truncation; safe-commands excludes Monica mutations, setup/config changes, uploads, and local exports.
Delivered lossless executable agent tool contracts and conservative mutation safety with complete local/live acceptance evidence.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: PR #63 exact-head CI diagnosis: Bun validation correctly rejected the changelog after master cut release 2026.7.22 while this branch was open. Merged current master, regenerated CHANGELOG.md with pm-changelog 2026.7.22, and reran bun run verify:release successfully at the synchronized state. This is expected stale-generated-artifact detection, not a pm-cli defect.

author: codex-root

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Hosted CI root cause: pm-changelog partitions items using Git release tags. Fetching origin/master alone did not fetch the newly created v2026.7.22 tag, so local generation temporarily grouped released items under Unreleased. Fetching the PR merge ref brought tags into parity with actions/checkout fetch-depth 0; regeneration now leaves only monica-agent-tool-contracts under Unreleased and restores the 2026.7.22 release section.

message: Record tag-aware changelog CI root cause
